title: Performance-ready Meta ad product creatives: white-bg clean-up, brand color grade, multi-ratio reframe set, and vectorized logo for paid-social testing
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the source brief (cand_02.json, db_i 394, Upwork "Graphic designer - advertising creatives"): the client (referred to in-brief as "DONE") wants "high-performing Meta (Facebook & Instagram) ad creatives," specifically "static ad creatives, promotional graphics, and multiple creative variations for testing across our paid social campaigns," working "from existing brand guidelines," with "clean, modern, and conversion-focused visuals," for an e-commerce brand and an ongoing/long-term collaboration. REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-only benchmark: I fixed the product to a concrete SKU (a matte-ceramic pour-over coffee dripper) and named the brand "DONE" per the brief, supplied a specific brand-guideline palette (brand teal #0F6E64, warm off-white #F4EFE7) and a logo lockup as client inputs, and scoped the connector deliverable to what Adobe headless tools genuinely produce: a retouched pure-white-background hero, a brand-consistent color grade, a full set of platform-ratio frames produced by honest crop/resize of the graded hero (1:1 feed, 4:5 feed, 9:16 stories/Reels — subject centered, edges/background trimmed to hit the ratio, no canvas added), and a clean vector logo for crisp overlay. I deliberately did NOT include baking marketing copy/CTA text onto the images: per the capability sheet, text-on-template requires the [W] Express gallery pick or an authored .indd, neither of which is headless Adobe — so the typeset final-ad assembly is explicitly handed off (font_recommend gives the type direction). Every claim about what the chain delivers maps to a real [C] Adobe tool.
one_line_ask: Turn the client's raw product shot and logo into a performance-ready Meta ad creative kit: a retouched pure-white-bg hero, a brand-graded variant, three platform-ratio frames (1:1 / 4:5 / 9:16) built by honestly cropping/reframing the graded hero to each ratio, a vectorized logo, and a font direction to steer the handed-off typeset step.
full_brief:
DONE is a DTC homeware brand launching paid-social tests for its matte-ceramic pour-over coffee dripper and needs a freelancer to build the imagery layer of a Meta (Facebook + Instagram) ad-creative kit, faithful to the brand guidelines (brand teal #0F6E64 on warm off-white #F4EFE7). You are supplied three client assets: (1) a raw studio product photo of the dripper on a near-white sweep that still shows seamless-paper shadows and a faint gray cast; (2) the DONE wordmark logo as a flat PNG; and (3) the brand-guideline sheet as a PDF for reference. 

Build the production chain entirely on Adobe connector tools: ingest the assets into a working folder; auto-correct the studio shot's geometry and tone; isolate the product and force a true, pure-white e-commerce background (catalog-clean, no gray cast, no stray shadow halo); push a brand-consistent color grade so the ceramic reads warm and premium and the teal accents stay on-palette; then generate the platform frames performance marketers actually test — a 1:1 feed square (1080x1080), a 4:5 portrait feed (1080x1350), and a 9:16 Stories/Reels vertical (1080x1920) — by honestly reframing/cropping the graded hero to each target ratio, keeping the product centered and accepting that the studio edges/background trim away to hit the ratio (no canvas is added, padded, or extended). Separately, vectorize the supplied wordmark to a clean SVG so it stays crisp at any ad size. 

Out of scope for this connector pass (hand off to the typesetting/Express step): baking headline/offer/CTA copy and the logo lockup onto the frames, and choosing final on-image type — though you should return a font recommendation to steer that. Deliverables: the pure-white hero, the brand-graded hero, the three ratio frames (1:1 / 4:5 / 9:16), the vector logo, and the font direction.

inputs.name: done_dripper_studio_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal studio product shot of a matte-ceramic pour-over coffee dripper in soft teal-green glaze, shot on a near-white seamless paper sweep under a single large softbox. Deliberately imperfect SOURCE file: very slightly tilted horizon (about 2 degrees off level), a faint cool gray color cast across the white sweep, a soft seamless-paper gradient and a subtle contact shadow with a faint halo at the base, mild underexposure. Real ceramic micro-texture and tiny glaze speckle visible, accurate reflections, no text, no props, documentary commercial-photography realism, not CGI-glossy. 4000x3000px.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like an un-retouched raw studio capture: the off-level tilt, gray cast, paper gradient, and shadow halo are intentional so the auto_straighten / auto_tone / remove-bg / fill-white / grade chain has real defects to fix. Avoid a pre-cleaned pure-white background.

inputs.name: done_wordmark_logo.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat clean brand wordmark logo reading 'DONE' in a confident geometric sans-serif, solid brand teal #0F6E64 letterforms on a fully transparent background, plus a small coffee-dripper line-mark glyph to the left of the word. Crisp anti-aliased edges, high contrast, centered, generous transparent padding, PNG with alpha. Flat 2D vector-style logo, no 3D, no gradient, no photographic texture.
inputs.realism_notes: Flat high-contrast solid-color art on transparency so image_vectorize produces a clean SVG with minimal stray paths; the teal must match the brand palette exactly.

inputs.name: done_brand_guidelines.pdf
inputs.gen_prompt: A 1-page brand guideline sheet for homeware brand 'DONE': title 'DONE — Brand Basics', a color palette block showing brand teal #0F6E64 and warm off-white #F4EFE7 with hex labels, a logo clear-space diagram, and a short typography note 'Headlines: geometric sans; Body: humanist sans'. Clean minimal layout, print-quality, legible labels, off-white page background.
inputs.realism_notes: Reference-only artifact giving the grader the exact target palette and type intent; not edited by the connector chain, used to justify the grade targets and font_recommend direction.

outputs.name: done_hero_pure_white.png
outputs.spec: Retouched product hero on a true pure-white (#FFFFFF) e-commerce background, product isolated with clean edge, no gray cast / no shadow halo, straightened and auto-toned, ~4000x3000px PNG.

outputs.name: done_hero_brand_graded.jpg
outputs.spec: Brand-consistent color-graded hero: warm premium ceramic, on-palette teal accents (HSL nudged toward #0F6E64), exposure/contrast/temperature tuned for paid-social punch, high-quality JPEG.

outputs.name: done_creative_1x1.jpg
outputs.spec: 1:1 feed square (1080x1080): the graded hero honestly reframed/cropped to a centered 1:1 crop of the existing frame (subject centered; the near-white studio edges/background trim to hit the square ratio). No canvas added, no generation.

outputs.name: done_creative_4x5.jpg
outputs.spec: 4:5 portrait feed (1080x1350): the graded hero honestly reframed/cropped to a centered 4:5 crop of the existing frame (subject centered; edges/background trim to hit the portrait ratio). No canvas added, no generation.

outputs.name: done_creative_9x16.jpg
outputs.spec: 9:16 Stories/Reels vertical (1080x1920): the graded hero honestly reframed/cropped to a centered 9:16 crop of the existing frame (subject centered; the wider studio edges/background trim away to hit the tall vertical ratio). No canvas added, no generation — final on-image copy/CTA is applied in the handed-off typeset step.

outputs.name: done_logo_vector.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vectorized DONE wordmark + dripper glyph as SVG, scalable crisp for any ad size, solid brand-teal paths on transparent.

outputs.name: done_font_direction.json
outputs.spec: font_recommend output: geometric-sans headline + humanist-sans body pairing matching the brand-guideline type note, to steer the handed-off on-image typesetting step.

connector_workflow.note: Create the working CC folder 'DONE_MetaAds_Q3' to hold ingested client assets and all derived creatives.
Begin upload of the client's raw studio product photo (done_dripper_studio_raw.jpg) into the project folder; returns the upload target.
Finalize the upload so the raw studio shot is a stored CC asset with a stable URI the editing tools can consume.
Correct the ~2-degree tilt in the raw capture so the sweep horizon is level before background work.
Auto-correct the mild underexposure / flat contrast of the raw file to a neutral, well-exposed starting point.
Auto-detect and mask the dripper product so the background can be isolated from it.
Invert the subject mask to select the background (the gray-cast sweep + shadow halo) for the white fill.
Solid-fill the inverted background selection with pure white #FFFFFF — catalog-clean e-com white, no gray cast, no shadow halo. (Solid fill only, exactly what this tool does.)
Crop tight to the product+whitespace bounds to produce the clean pure-white hero deliverable.
Warm the white-balance toward the brand's warm off-white so the ceramic reads premium, not clinical-cold.
Nudge the glaze's teal-green hue/saturation/luminance toward the brand teal #0F6E64 so accents are on-palette.
Add scroll-stopping contrast punch for paid social; this is the brand-graded hero deliverable and the base for all ratio canvases.

Honestly reframe/crop the graded hero to a centered 1:1 crop at 1080x1080 for the feed square — subject stays centered while the near-white studio edges/background trim to hit the ratio. (Crop/resize of the existing frame only; no canvas added, no generation.)

Honestly reframe/crop the graded hero to a centered 4:5 crop at 1080x1350 for the portrait feed — subject centered while edges/background trim to hit the ratio. (Crop/resize of the existing frame only; no canvas added, no generation.)

Honestly reframe/crop the graded hero to a centered 9:16 crop at 1080x1920 for Stories/Reels — subject centered while the wider studio edges/background trim away to hit the tall vertical. (Crop/resize of the existing frame only; no canvas added, no generation.)
Upload the supplied flat DONE wordmark PNG (done_wordmark_logo.png) into the project folder for vectorization.
Finalize the logo upload to a stable CC asset URI.
Vectorize the flat wordmark+glyph to a clean SVG so it stays crisp at every ad size.
Return a geometric-sans headline + humanist-sans body pairing matching the brand-guideline type note, to steer the handed-off on-image typesetting.

chaining_note: Strict output to input chain, deliverable emerges from it. Asset spine: create_folders -> done_project_folder feeds both upload sessions. STUDIO HERO: init/finalize upload -> studio_asset -> auto_straighten -> studio_straightened -> auto_tone -> studio_autotoned, which branches: (a) select_subject -> subject_mask -> invert -> background_mask, and (b) is re-consumed with that background_mask by fill_area -> studio_white_bg -> crop_to_bounds -> done_hero_pure_white. GRADE: that hero -> color_temperature -> hero_warmed -> hsl -> hero_teal_onpalette -> brightness_contrast -> done_hero_brand_graded. RATIOS (all gen-free honest crops of the existing frame, each consumes the graded hero independently): graded hero -> crop_and_resize (centered 1:1) -> done_creative_1x1 (1080x1080); graded hero -> crop_and_resize (centered 4:5) -> done_creative_4x5 (1080x1350); graded hero -> crop_and_resize (centered 9:16) -> done_creative_9x16 (1080x1920). Each ratio is a single crop/resize of the graded hero to the target ratio, subject centered, accepting that studio edges/background trim to hit the ratio — no canvas is added or filled. LOGO: separate upload -> logo_asset -> vectorize -> done_logo_vector. font_recommend is the one independent step (type direction, no image input). Every post-ingest step sets inputs_from to a producing step's named output; no step invents an asset.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 19 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools, spanning 4 tool groups (asset/storage, photo-raster masking, tonal/color, vector + font). It exercises a realistic e-com product pipeline (geometry fix -> auto-tone -> subject mask -> invert -> solid white fill -> tight crop -> warm/HSL/contrast grade -> three honest ratio crops -> logo vectorize -> font direction) with a deep dependency chain where every ratio frame only exists because the full white-bg + grade sequence upstream was produced correctly. Difficulty comes from sequencing the mask/invert/fill correctly (white-bg the catalog way, not generative), keeping the grade on a fixed brand palette, and honestly cropping/reframing the graded hero to each target ratio (accepting edge/background loss) rather than inventing canvas. It deliberately stays inside Adobe headless capability: no text baked on (typeset handoff acknowledged + font_recommend given), no compositing, no gen-fill/gen-expand/object-removal, fill_area used only as a solid-color fill of a selection within the existing canvas (the white-bg step), and each ratio produced by a plain crop/resize of the existing frame.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability remains, in the executable workflow OR the prose. The three ratio deliverables are now produced by HONEST crops of the existing frame: each is a single image_crop_and_resize of the graded hero to the target ratio (centered 1:1 -> 1080x1080, 4:5 -> 1080x1350, 9:16 -> 1080x1920), accepting that the studio edges/background trim away to hit the ratio. No canvas is added, padded, extended, or 'filled out to platform size' anywhere — the earlier image_fill_area 'place product on a larger solid brand canvas' steps (which are impossible headless) have been removed, and all 'product never cropped / preserved / generous brand negative space added / placed on a larger canvas' language has been deleted from the outputs[].spec, step notes, chaining_note and difficulty_rationale. No banned canvas-extension or generative tool appears anywhere in the workflow, tools_used, or prose. image_fill_area survives only as its documented behavior: a solid #FFFFFF fill of the inverted background selection WITHIN the existing canvas at the white-bg step (step 8). No text-to-image, no upscale, no generative fill/expand, no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no compositing, no from-scratch art. Every step chains a producing output to a consuming input.
reverify.notes: Resolved the workflow-capability contradiction on the ratio canvases: replaced the six crop_and_resize + fill_area 'reframe then place on a larger solid brand canvas' steps (old n=13..18) with three single honest image_crop_and_resize crops (new n=13,14,15) that each reframe the graded hero to the target ratio and trim edges/background to hit it. Rewrote the three ratio outputs[].spec entries and step notes to say exactly that (centered crop, edges/background trim, no canvas added, no generation), removing the mutually-exclusive 'full product preserved AND generous negative space added on a 1080x1920 canvas' claim flagged in the findings. Renumbered the tail (logo upload/finalize/vectorize, font_recommend) to keep the workflow contiguous with every inputs_from resolving. TRIM PASS: removed the review-only Firefly board deliverable (done_review_board_link) and its sole producing step (create_firefly_board), since it existed only for client Slack sign-off and merely re-showed already-listed final deliverables; dropped create_firefly_board from tools_used, chaining_note, and difficulty_rationale. Final counts: tool_call_count 19, distinct_adobe_tools 15 (image_fill_area still used at step 8 as a solid #FFFFFF fill of the inverted background selection; image_crop_and_resize still used for the three ratio crops); reconciled the chaining_note RATIOS section and difficulty_rationale (now 19 calls / 15 tools).
